codeReview.summary: The commit introduces an injectable foreign persistence abstraction and threads it through resolve_foreign_record_id, but it stops at a generic Repo-backed adapter and a unit-style seam test. It does not deliver the feature’s required transaction-aware foundation because the new abstraction cannot operate on a live save/save_many transaction handle and the added coverage never exercises parent save/save_many through the seam.

issues.description: The new ForeignPersistence seam is still fundamentally repository-scoped: its only operations are exists_record/create/create_at, and the default implementation calls repository::record_exists / Repo::<T>::create / Repo::<T>::create_at. There is no capability to bind the seam to the authoritative uncommitted transaction handle used by parent save/save_many writes, so missing foreign children still cannot be resolved/created inside that same transaction boundary as required by the feature description.

The only new regression test calls resolve_foreign_record_id_with directly with a recording adapter and asserts event logging. It does not drive Repo::save or Repo::save_many, does not prove the seam is reusable by those code paths, and does not verify behavior against a live transaction-owned parent write. As a result, the implementation is not demonstrated as the reusable save-atomicity foundation the feature requires.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission library documents that save/save_many now require transactional parent/child semantics, but it does not explicitly record the more specific acceptance criterion for seam-only foundation work: the foreign persistence context must be able to target the same live uncommitted Surreal transaction handle as parent writes, not just an abstract repository adapter.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md and nested-store.md describe transactional semantics broadly, while this failed feature implemented a repo-backed injection seam in core/src/lib.rs:135-177 instead of a transaction-bound context.

summary: Review failed: the commit adds an injectable repo-level foreign persistence adapter, but it does not provide or prove the transaction-bound seam required for later atomic save/save_many work.
